# SHA Family Algorithm Example in Scala

Here's an example demonstrating SHA-256 hashing using Scala:

```scala
import java.security.MessageDigest
import scala.util.Try

object SHAExample {
  
  def sha256(input: String): String = {
    val digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256")
    val hashBytes = digest.digest(input.getBytes("UTF-8"))
    
    // Convert bytes to hexadecimal string
    hashBytes.map("%02x".format(_)).mkString
  }
  
  def sha1(input: String): String = {
    val digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-1")
    val hashBytes = digest.digest(input.getBytes("UTF-8"))
    
    hashBytes.map("%02x".format(_)).mkString
  }
  
  def sha512(input: String): String = {
    val digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-512")
    val hashBytes = digest.digest(input.getBytes("UTF-8"))
    
    hashBytes.map("%02x".format(_)).mkString
  }
  
  def main(args: Array[String]): Unit = {
    val text = "Hello, World!"
    
    println(s"Original text: $text")
    println(s"SHA-256: ${sha256(text)}")
    println(s"SHA-1: ${sha1(text)}")
    println(s"SHA-512: ${sha512(text)}")
    
    // Example with Try for error handling
    val result = Try {
      sha256("Sample text")
    }
    
    result.foreach(hash => println(s"Hash: $hash"))
  }
}
```

## Output Example:
```
Original text: Hello, World!
SHA-256: dffd6021bb2bd5b0af676290809ec3a53191dd81c7f70a4b28688a362182986f
SHA-1: 2ef7bde608ce5404e97d5f042f95f89f1c231f04
SHA-512:
